zh.Thaxted, Uttlesford district, Essex, Eng: 英國英格蘭艾可塔可泰德
zh.lifeSummary: 當James Parker於1535年出生在英國英格蘭戴文格奧智哈姆時，他的父親James Parker27歲，母親Mrs. James Parker23歲。他於1559年10月15日與Margery Patch在英國艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1603年12月在英國英格蘭艾可塔可泰德享年68歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可塔可泰德。

nameMeanings.name: Parker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English parker ‘park-keeper’ (Old French parquier, parchier), an officer employed to look after deer and other game in a hunting park (see Park 1). This surname is also very common among African Americans. It has also been recorded since medieval times in Ireland.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

variants.name: Parkman
meanings: occupational name from Middle English park ‘enclosure, game preserve, park’ (Old French parc) + man, sometimes for one living at or near such a place but probably more often denoting someone employed to look after after deer and other game in a hunting park; compare Parker .
occupational name denoting the servant (Middle English man) of someone called Park (see Park 2).

variants.name: Parkerson
meanings: English: from the occupational name Parker + patronymic -son.

variants.name: Parkey
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): dialect pronunciation of Parker . Parker has a long history in Macclesfield, Manchester, and Bridgnorth.

variants.name: Park
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Sang, Sung, Yong, Jung, Jong, Chan, Jae, Jin, Kwang, Kyung, Myung, Chong, Chang, Chung, Byung, Min, Moon, Chul, Hae, Jeong, Dae, Seong, Myong.

English and Scottish: from Middle English and Older Scots parc, parke, perk ‘park, enclosure’ (Old French parc), often referring to the grounds of a manor house or a deer park where the lord hunted. The name was probably synonymous with Parker , denoting an officer who looked after a park for the lord of the manor.
English and Scottish: from a medieval pet form of the personal name   Peter  . Compare   Parkin  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Parker was born in 1535, in Georgeham, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, James Parker, was 27 and his mother, Mrs. James Parker, was 23. He married Margery Patch on 15 October 1559, in Great Burstead, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in December 1603, in Thaxted, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68, and was buried in Thaxted, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: James Parker(이)가 1535년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 조그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Parker(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. James Parker(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1559년 10월 15일 영국 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 Margery Patch(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1603년 12월 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스액테드에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스액테드에 묻혔다.

Он умер декабрь 1603 г. в местности такстед, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности такстед, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
